children.heading: .—Information relating to basis of rate
children.label: 1 .—Information relating to basis of rate
children.text: A motor carrier of property (other than a motor carrier providing transportation in noncontiguous domestic trade) shall provide to the shipper, on request of the shipper, a written or electronic copy of the rate, classification, rules, and practices, upon which any rate applicable to its shipment or agreed to between the shipper and carrier is based.

children.heading: .—Reasonableness of rates; collecting additional charges
children.label: 2 .—Reasonableness of rates; collecting additional charges
children.text: When the applicability or reasonableness of the rates and related provisions billed by a motor carrier is challenged by the person paying the freight charges, the Board shall determine whether such rates and provisions are reasonable under section 13701 or applicable based on the record before it.

children.heading: .—Initiated by motor carriers
children.label: A .—Initiated by motor carriers
children.text: In those cases where a motor carrier (other than a motor carrier providing transportation of household goods or in noncontiguous domestic trade) seeks to collect charges in addition to those billed and collected which are contested by the payor, the carrier may request that the Board determine whether any additional charges over those billed and collected must be paid. A carrier must issue any bill for charges in addition to those originally billed within 180 days of the receipt of the original bill in order to have the right to collect such charges.

children.heading: .—Initiated by shippers
children.label: B .—Initiated by shippers
children.text: If a shipper seeks to contest the charges originally billed or additional charges subsequently billed, the shipper may request that the Board determine whether the charges billed must be paid. A shipper must contest the original bill or subsequent bill within 180 days of receipt of the bill in order to have the right to contest such charges.

children.heading: .—Voiding of certain tariffs
children.label: 4 .—Voiding of certain tariffs
children.text: Any tariff on file with the Interstate Commerce Commission on , and not required to be filed after that date is null and void beginning on that date. Any tariff on file with the Interstate Commerce Commission on , and not required to be filed after that date is null and void beginning on that date.August 26, 1994January 1, 1996

children.heading: .—Resolution of Disputes Over Status of Common Carrier or Contract Carrier
children.label: b .—Resolution of Disputes Over Status of Common Carrier or Contract Carrier
children.text: If a motor carrier (other than a motor carrier providing transportation of household goods) that was subject to jurisdiction under subchapter II of chapter 105, as in effect on , and that had authority to provide transportation as both a motor common carrier and a motor contract carrier and a dispute arises as to whether certain transportation that was provided prior to , was provided in its common carrier or contract carrier capacity and the parties are not able to resolve the dispute consensually, the Board shall resolve the dispute.December 31, 1995January 1, 1996
